user_message: I need to identify the blockers for the Iron Creek Mining engagement. I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-18 10:52 EST
Subject: Re: Re: whitespace analysis Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-22 13:01 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the whitespace analysis proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 14:10 EST
Subject: Re: whitespace analysis Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-01-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:06:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:09:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:11:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:19:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the whitespace analysis front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:27:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:30:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is budget approval.

[00:34:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:38:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
